title: andropogon glaucescens subvar. typicus
intro: Andropogon glaucescens subvar. typicus, also known as Buffalo Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Great Plains region of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Buffalo Grass include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Buffalo Grass is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and can be dried to be used in smudging ceremonies for its purifying properties.
